topic: skill writing best practices for AI agents, especially review/code-review skills

sources.title: Agent Skills Overview — Anthropic Official Docs
keyInsights: Three-level loading model: metadata (~100 tokens), instructions (<5k tokens), resources (unlimited)
YAML frontmatter required fields: name (max 64 chars, lowercase/hyphens) and description (max 1024 chars)
Description must describe both what the skill does and when to use it
Skills support auto-invocation vs manual invocation via disable-model-invocation flag
Skill content lifecycle: once loaded, stays in context through compaction (25k combined budget)

sources.title: Skill Authoring Best Practices — Anthropic Official Docs
keyInsights: Concise is key: only add context Claude doesn't already have
Description must be third-person; first-person causes discovery problems
Evaluation-driven development: build evals BEFORE writing extensive documentation
Match specificity to fragility: high freedom for flexible tasks, low freedom for fragile operations
Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines; split into supporting files using progressive disclosure
Iterative development: Claude A creates/refines, Claude B tests in real usage
Anti-pattern: offering too many options; provide a default with an escape hatch

sources.title: Extend Claude with Skills — Claude Code Official Docs
keyInsights: Claude Code-specific fields: when_to_use, disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, paths, context, agent, model, effort
when_to_use is appended to description; combined text capped at 1536 chars in skill listing
Dynamic context injection via !`command` syntax for live data (git diff, PR info, etc.)
context: fork runs skill in isolated subagent; agent field specifies subagent type
paths: glob patterns restrict auto-activation to matching files (ideal for language-specific skills)
Skill content lifecycle: stays in context after first load; re-attached after compaction (first 5k tokens each, 25k combined budget)
skillListingBudgetFraction and maxSkillDescriptionChars settings for tuning description budget

sources.title: Best Practices for Claude Code — Official Docs
keyInsights: Skills vs CLAUDE.md: skills load on demand; CLAUDE.md loads every session
Use skills for domain knowledge and workflows that only apply sometimes
Conciseness test for CLAUDE.md applies equally to skills
Skills extend Claude's knowledge with project/team/domain-specific info

sources.title: Agent Skills Open Standard Specification
keyInsights: Cross-agent open standard: works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, etc.
name field: 1-64 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, no consecutive --, must match directory name
description field: 1-1024 chars, should describe both what and when
Optional fields: license, compatibility (max 500 chars), metadata (arbitrary key-value), allowed-tools
Progressive disclosure: metadata ~100 tokens, instructions <5000 tokens recommended, resources unlimited
Body content: no format restrictions; recommended sections: step-by-step, examples, edge cases
Validation tool: skills-ref CLI validates frontmatter

sources.title: Best Practices for Skill Creators — agentskills.io
keyInsights: Start from real expertise, not LLM-generated generic content
Gotchas sections: environment-specific facts that defy reasonable assumptions — highest ROI
Procedures over declarations: teach how to approach a class of problems, not what to produce
Validation loops: do work, run validator, fix, repeat until passes
Plan-validate-execute pattern for batch/destructive operations
Calibrating control: match specificity to fragility; explain why for flexible instructions
Provide defaults, not menus: pick one approach with a brief escape hatch

sources.title: Optimizing Skill Descriptions — agentskills.io
keyInsights: Use imperative phrasing: 'Use this skill when...' not 'This skill does...'
Focus on user intent, not implementation details
Err on the side of being pushy: explicitly list non-obvious trigger contexts
Trigger eval methodology: 20 queries (10 positive, 10 near-miss negatives), run each 3 times, target trigger rate
Should-not-trigger queries: most valuable are near-misses (share keywords but different task)
Train/validation split (60/40): optimize on train set, validate generalization on held-out set
Optimization loop: evaluate, identify failures, revise description, repeat (5 iterations usually enough)
Before: 'Process CSV files.' After: comprehensive description with task vocabulary and non-obvious triggers

sources.title: Agent Skills Quickstart — agentskills.io
keyInsights: Minimal viable skill: name + description frontmatter + body < 20 lines
Discovery → Activation → Execution: the three-step progressive disclosure flow
Tool-use reliability varies across models; some activate more consistently than others

sources.title: Agent Skills — Open Standard Overview
keyInsights: Cross-agent adoption: Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, VS Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Roo Code, Factory, Junie (JetBrains), and many more
Standard originally developed by Anthropic, now open to ecosystem contributions
Portability: same SKILL.md works across all compatible agents

sources.title: Equipping Agents for the Real World with Agent Skills — Anthropic Engineering Blog
keyInsights: Design rationale: filesystem-based architecture enables progressive disclosure without practical context limit
Dual-mode capabilities: instructional documentation + executable code in same skill
Evaluation methodology: identify gaps, build evaluations, measure baseline, write minimal instructions, iterate
Skills metadata is the discovery gate; body is only loaded when metadata matches
Claude A / Claude B iteration pattern: one instance refines, another tests in real usage

sources.title: anthropics/skills — Open-Source Skills Library (GitHub)
keyInsights: Available skills: claude-api, mcp-builder, webapp-testing, skill-creator, algorithmic-art, canvas-design, frontend-design, theme-factory, brand-guidelines, docx, pptx, xlsx, pdf, doc-coauthoring, slack-gif-creator, internal-comms
skill-creator meta-skill: automates trigger eval loop end-to-end with train/validation split and HTML report
Self-contained folders, metadata-first, example-based, guideline-based patterns

sources.title: Agent Skills Cookbook — Anthropic Platform
keyInsights: API usage: skills loaded via container.skills array with skill_id, type: anthropic/custom, version
Three beta headers required: code-execution-2025-08-25, files-api-2025-04-14, skills-2025-10-02
Version pinning: 'latest' for Anthropic-maintained skills; epoch timestamp for custom skills
Custom skills are workspace-wide in the API; individual in claude.ai

sources.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — AWS Documentation
keyInsights: AgentCore provides managed memory, gateway (tool connectivity), identity, policy, code interpreter, browser runtime
Memory service: persistent context across agent interactions
Gateway: semantic tool discovery, converts APIs and Lambda functions into agent-ready capabilities
Note: AWS AgentCore docs did not render via WebFetch; insights from marketing page and indirect sources only

sources.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — AWS Product Page
keyInsights: Memory service: cross-session context persistence with semantic search for retrieval
Skills registry pattern: agent skills stored as descriptorType: AGENT_SKILLS MemoryRecords
SearchMemoryRecords uses semantic similarity over combined name + description + body text
Note: Detail on SkillMdConfiguration and inlineContent structure from indirect AWS sources; official API reference was inaccessible

sources.title: Using Qodo Agent Skills to Auto-Fix PR Issues — Qodo Blog
keyInsights: Review skill pattern: structured issue context includes severity (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), category, exact code location, agent prompt per issue
Two complementary skills: get-qodo-rules (fetch standards before generation), qodo-pr-resolver (fix identified issues)
Batch vs interactive mode for handling multiple review comments
GitHub CLI integration for automated inline comment replies
Installation via npx: skills as installable packages

sources.title: Factory.ai Skills Configuration — Factory Docs
keyInsights: Enterprise skill structure: instructions + inputs + success criteria + verification + fallbacks + constraints
Design principles: narrow and outcome-focused, idempotent, encode team conventions, machine-parseable output
Workspace vs personal skill scoping; disable-model-invocation supported

sources.title: Roo Code Skills — Roo Code Docs
keyInsights: Mode-specific skills: skills-code/, skills-architect/, etc. for mode-scoped activation
Progressive disclosure: same three-level model as Claude Code
Description specificity requirement: vague descriptions trigger unreliably across models

sources.title: Cursor Agent Skills — Cursor Docs
keyInsights: Cursor looks for skills in .agents/skills/, .cursor/skills/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/
paths field restricts auto-activation to file patterns matching glob
Compatible with Claude Code .claude/skills/ format via cross-agent standard

sources.title: About Agent Skills — GitHub Copilot Docs
keyInsights: GitHub Copilot skill directories: .github/skills/, .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/ (project); ~/.copilot/skills/, ~/.agents/skills/ (personal)
Discovery via 'gh skill' CLI command and community repositories (anthropics/skills, github/awesome-copilot)
Same open standard as Claude Code; portability across agent platforms

sources.title: Ona Skills for Repository Workflows — Ona Docs
keyInsights: Skills as repeatable multi-step procedures: deployment workflows, triage runbooks, PR checklists
Repository skills vs organization skills: project-scoped vs cross-project
Skills complement AGENTS.md (static context) for complex procedures

sources.title: anthropics/skills Tree — GitHub
keyInsights: Available categories: Creative & Design, Development & Technical, Enterprise & Communication, Document Skills
Development skills: claude-api, mcp-builder, webapp-testing, skill-creator
Each skill is a self-contained directory; Apache 2.0 license for most

sources.title: valkey-review/config/skill.md — Production Review Skill (local)
keyInsights: Production review skill structure: operating rules → always-on checks → grep hazards → iterator-invariant taxonomy → subsystem patterns
Each pattern follows: 'diff looks OK but is wrong when' / 'reviewer memory' / 'cites' / 'skip unless' template
Confidence ladder: high (3 citation types), medium (2 types), low (drop)
Always-on checks are cheap/deterministic and do not count against confidence budget
Grep hazards section prevents noise on Redis-era identifiers intentionally retained for compat
35KB / ~900 lines justified by institutional memory density; each pattern gates on specific identifiers
Description includes explicit 'Not for authoring' scope exclusion and 'Redis-baseline assumed' exclusion

gaps: AWS AgentCore SkillMdConfiguration API details not directly retrievable (docs returned empty); described from indirect sources and general knowledge
No direct access to webapp-testing or mcp-builder SKILL.md content from anthropics/skills (404s)
Spring AI and Databricks Genie Code skill implementations not analyzed in depth
